g.data-narration: Reputation-guided protection assigns trust scores to digital entities like IPs and URLs, using those scores to make real-time access decisions for data centers.
Reputation works like a credit score for digital entities. High scores mean trustworthy behavior, while low scores signal potential threats that should be blocked.
g.text: Think: Credit Score for Digital Entities

g.data-narration: Systems gather data from internal logs and external threat feeds like Cisco Talos. This data flows to the scoring engine which calculates reputation in real-time.
The scoring engine analyzes multiple factors to assign reputation scores. Static scoring checks blacklists, while dynamic scoring uses real-time behavior and machine learning.
Based on reputation scores, the system takes different actions. High scores get fast access, medium scores face extra checks, and low scores are blocked immediately.
During DDoS attacks, reputation identifies botnet traffic by low scores. Legitimate users with high scores continue unaffected while malicious traffic gets blocked.
g.text: Proactive: blocks attack before payload executes; legitimate traffic unaffected

g.data-narration: Major providers offer reputation-based protection: AWS Shield, Azure DDoS Protection, and Cloudflare all use global threat intelligence to filter traffic at the edge.
The system continuously learns by monitoring outcomes, analyzing mistakes, adjusting algorithms, and sharing intelligence with the global security community.
Key advantages: proactive defense, adaptive scaling, reduced false positives, resource optimization, and shared global intelligence for better protection.
